result.summary: metastore.googleapis.com exposes the Dataproc Metastore HTTP API at https://metastore.googleapis.com/ and a corresponding gcloud metastore CLI surface; auth is via Google Cloud OAuth access tokens or service-account-derived bearer tokens, and discovery docs also show API key query support.
result.description: Dataproc Metastore is Google Cloud’s managed Apache Hive metastore service for managing metastore services, federations, backups, and related metadata operations. It is used to provision and administer metastore infrastructure for data processing workloads on Google Cloud.

gcp_api_key.label: Google Cloud API key
gcp_api_key.setup: Create an API key in Google Cloud Console following [Manage API keys](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys). The key is sent to Google APIs as the `key` query parameter; [Use API keys to access APIs](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/api-keys-use) notes that only APIs that accept API keys can use this method.

gcp_oauth_access_token.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth_access_token.setup: Authenticate with Google Cloud using [gcloud auth login](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/login) for a user account, then obtain an access token with `gcloud auth print-access-token`; or set up Application Default Credentials with [gcloud auth application-default login](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/application-default/login) as described in [Set up Application Default Credentials](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc). The Dataproc Metastore discovery document lists the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform` OAuth scope on methods.

gcp_service_account.label: Google Cloud service account credentials
gcp_service_account.setup: Create or use a Google Cloud service account and authenticate with its key using [gcloud auth activate-service-account](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/auth/activate-service-account), or use service-account-based Application Default Credentials as described in [Set up Application Default Credentials](https://docs.cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc). Use the resulting OAuth 2.0 access token when calling Google Cloud APIs.
